seoTitle: Microchip Solutions | IoT, Industrial, Automotive Applications | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Explore Microchip application solutions for IoT connectivity, industrial automation, automotive electronics, and embedded control. Complete reference designs and technical support.

faqs.question: What types of solutions does Microchip offer?
faqs.answer: Microchip provides comprehensive solutions across multiple market segments: 1) IoT and Connectivity solutions featuring wireless sensor networks, cloud-connected devices, and smart home applications using WiFi, Bluetooth, LoRa, and Zigbee technologies; 2) Industrial Automation solutions including motor control, PLC systems, industrial networking with CAN and Ethernet, and factory automation; 3) Automotive Electronics solutions covering body electronics, powertrain control, infotainment systems, and ADAS applications with AEC-Q100 qualified components; 4) Consumer Electronics solutions for wearables, smart appliances, and battery-powered devices; 5) Aerospace and Defense solutions with radiation-tolerant FPGAs, high-reliability microcontrollers, and secure communication systems. Each solution includes reference designs, software libraries, and technical documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our solutions by application area or contact our FAE team for solution recommendations tailored to your specific requirements.

faqs.question: How can I get started with Microchip reference designs?
faqs.answer: Getting started with Microchip reference designs is straightforward: 1) Browse our solutions catalog to find a reference design matching your application requirements; 2) Download the complete design package including schematics, PCB layout, BOM, and source code from our website; 3) Order the associated development board or evaluation kit to evaluate the solution; 4) Review the application note and user guide for detailed implementation guidance; 5) Contact our FAE team for technical support during your evaluation and customization. Many reference designs are available as complete turnkey solutions that can be directly adapted to your product. We also offer customization services to modify reference designs for specific customer requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with the reference design closest to your application, evaluate on development hardware, then customize for your specific requirements.

faqs.question: Does Microchip provide software support for their solutions?
faqs.answer: Yes, Microchip provides comprehensive software support for their solutions: 1) MPLAB Harmony - modular firmware framework for 32-bit SAM and PIC32 applications with middleware, drivers, and RTOS integration; 2) MPLAB Code Configurator (MCC) - graphical code generation tool for 8-bit and 16-bit PIC microcontrollers; 3) Atmel START - web-based code configurator for SAM and AVR microcontrollers; 4) FreeRTOS and ThreadX ports optimized for Microchip devices; 5) Extensive library support for wireless connectivity, motor control, digital power, and security; 6) Application-specific software stacks for USB, TCP/IP, Bluetooth, and WiFi; 7) Security solutions including crypto libraries and secure boot implementations. All software is provided with source code and comprehensive documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MPLAB Harmony for 32-bit applications, MCC for 8/16-bit PIC, and Atmel START for SAM/AVR devices.

faqs.question: What support is available for customizing Microchip solutions?
faqs.answer: Microchip and authorized distributors like BeiLuo provide extensive support for solution customization: 1) FAE consultation to review your requirements and recommend optimal architectures; 2) Schematic and PCB layout review services to ensure robust designs; 3) Software customization support including driver modifications and application-specific firmware development; 4) Access to Microchip's design centers for complex customizations; 5) Training and workshops on Microchip products and development tools; 6) Prototype programming and testing services; 7) Long-term supply agreements for production volumes. Our technical team can help adapt reference designs to your specific mechanical, electrical, and environmental requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team early in your design cycle for architecture guidance and customization support.

faqs.question: Are Microchip solutions suitable for safety-critical applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Microchip offers solutions specifically designed for safety-critical applications: 1) Functional safety support with FMEDA documentation and safety manuals for ISO 26262 (automotive) and IEC 61508 (industrial) compliance; 2) Hardware safety features including ECC memory, clock monitoring, and dual-core lockstep in dsPIC33C devices; 3) Software libraries certified to safety standards; 4) Radiation-tolerant FPGAs for aerospace applications; 5) Secure boot and crypto services for security-critical systems; 6) Long product lifecycles ensuring supply continuity for safety-certified designs. Microchip's functional safety packages include detailed analysis of failure modes, diagnostic coverage, and safety mechanism documentation required for certification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our functional safety specialists for safety documentation and MCU recommendations for your safety-critical application.

solutions.title: Low-Power IoT Sensor Node
solutions.description: Complete battery-powered IoT sensor node solution with wireless connectivity, ultra-low power consumption, and cloud integration.
solutions.longDescription:
The Industrial Control Solution 1 from microchip represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of microchip components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.
features: 5+ year battery life on 2x AA batteries
WiFi and Bluetooth Low Energy connectivity
benefits: Rapid time-to-market with complete reference design
Ultra-low power consumption extends battery life
Secure cloud connectivity out of the box
Modular design enables easy sensor customization
Comprehensive software stack reduces development effort

customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement with enhanced reliability.

faeInsights.title: Power Optimization Techniques
faeInsights.content: To achieve maximum battery life, implement a tiered sleep strategy: Use STANDBY mode with RTC for periodic wake-ups (2.5μA), IDLE mode when waiting for ADC conversions (1.2mA), and ACTIVE mode only during wireless transmission. Disable all unused peripherals and clock domains. Use the event system to trigger ADC conversions without CPU intervention. Schedule transmissions in bursts to minimize the high-current WiFi association time. With these optimizations, we've achieved 6+ years on 2x AA batteries with hourly sensor readings.
faeInsights.highlight: Tiered sleep strategy and event system usage are critical for achieving 5+ year battery life

faeInsights.title: Antenna Design Considerations
faeInsights.content: The ATWINC3400 module includes an integrated PCB antenna that works well for most applications. However, for designs requiring extended range or operation inside metal enclosures, consider an external antenna via the U.FL connector. Keep the antenna area clear of copper pours and high-frequency signals. For agricultural or industrial applications with harsh environments, use an external IP67-rated antenna. Always perform range testing in the actual deployment environment as building materials and interference sources significantly affect performance.
faeInsights.highlight: Integrated antenna works for most apps; use external antenna for metal enclosures or extended range

faqs.question: What is the typical battery life for this sensor node?
faqs.answer: Battery life depends heavily on the transmission frequency and sensor types used. With the standard configuration (2x AA alkaline batteries, temperature/humidity sensor, hourly WiFi transmission), expect 5-6 years of operation. Increasing transmission frequency to every 15 minutes reduces battery life to approximately 2 years. Using energy harvesting (solar or vibration) can extend operation indefinitely. For longest battery life: 1) Use STANDBY sleep mode between transmissions; 2) Batch sensor readings and transmit in bursts; 3) Disable Bluetooth when only WiFi is needed; 4) Use the event system to minimize CPU wake time; 5) Implement adaptive transmission frequency based on sensor value changes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use hourly transmission for 5+ year battery life; consider energy harvesting for more frequent updates.

faqs.question: How do I connect this sensor node to my cloud platform?
faqs.answer: The reference design includes a complete AWS IoT Core integration with MQTT protocol. To connect to your own cloud platform: 1) Modify the cloud_config.h file with your server endpoint, port, and credentials; 2) Implement the required TLS/SSL certificates for secure communication; 3) Customize the JSON payload format to match your cloud API; 4) Update the OTA update endpoint for your infrastructure. The software stack supports MQTT, HTTPS, and CoAP protocols. For Azure IoT Hub or Google Cloud IoT Core, use the provided TLS libraries and adapt the connection handshake. Our FAE team can provide integration support for major cloud platforms.
faqs.decisionGuide: AWS IoT Core is pre-integrated; contact FAE for Azure, Google Cloud, or private cloud integration support.

faqs.question: Can I add additional sensors to this reference design?
faqs.answer: Yes, the reference design includes multiple sensor interfaces for expansion: 1) I2C bus with two available addresses for digital sensors (humidity, pressure, light); 2) SPI interface for high-speed sensors or SD card storage; 3) 8-channel 12-bit ADC for analog sensors (soil moisture, gas sensors, strain gauges); 4) UART for GPS modules or serial sensors; 5) GPIOs for digital sensors and interrupts. The software stack includes drivers for common sensors. When adding sensors, consider power consumption impact on battery life and update the enclosure design for sensor exposure. Our FAE team can review your sensor requirements and recommend optimal integration approaches.
faqs.decisionGuide: I2C and ADC interfaces available for expansion; evaluate power impact of additional sensors on battery life.

faqs.question: What security features are included in this solution?
faqs.answer: The solution includes comprehensive security features: 1) ATECC608A secure element provides hardware-based key storage, ECDSA signatures, and SHA-256 hashing; 2) Unique device certificates pre-provisioned during manufacturing; 3) TLS 1.2 encryption for all cloud communications; 4) Secure boot prevents unauthorized firmware execution; 5) Hardware tamper detection with secure key destruction; 6) OTA firmware updates with cryptographic verification. The secure element ensures private keys never leave the hardware, protecting against software attacks. For additional security, the solution supports encrypted sensor data and mutual authentication with the cloud server.
faqs.decisionGuide: ATECC608A secure element provides enterprise-grade security; no additional security components needed for most IoT applications.

faqs.question: Is this solution suitable for outdoor deployment?
faqs.answer: The base reference design is rated for industrial temperature range (-40°C to +85°C) but is not weatherproof. For outdoor deployment, you'll need an IP65 or IP67 rated enclosure with appropriate cable glands for sensor connections. Consider these modifications: 1) Use conformal coating on the PCB for moisture protection; 2) Add a desiccant packet inside the enclosure; 3) Use an external antenna with weatherproof connector; 4) Ensure battery compartment is sealed but vented for pressure equalization; 5) Consider solar panel integration for energy harvesting in sunny locations. The electronics are suitable for outdoor use, but proper enclosure design is critical for long-term reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Electronics rated for outdoor temperatures; use IP65+ enclosure and conformal coating for weatherproofing.

faeInsights.title: FOC Tuning Best Practices
faeInsights.content: Start tuning with the Motor Control Tuning GUI in MPLAB X. First, characterize your motor parameters (Rs, Ld, Lq, Ke) using the automated measurement tool. Set current loop bandwidth to 1/10th of switching frequency (2kHz for 20kHz PWM). Set speed loop bandwidth to 1/10th of current loop (200Hz). Start with conservative PI gains and gradually increase while monitoring for oscillations. Use the real-time scope to observe current waveforms - they should be clean sinusoids with minimal distortion. For sensorless operation, ensure the observer converges at low speeds before enabling closed-loop control.
faeInsights.highlight: Use automated motor characterization; set current loop 10x faster than speed loop for stable operation

faeInsights.title: Thermal Management
faeInsights.content: The MOSFETs and gate driver are the primary heat sources. For continuous operation above 500W, implement a heatsink with thermal interface material. Monitor MOSFET temperature with the onboard thermistor and implement thermal derating - reduce current limit when temperature exceeds 80°C. The dsPIC33CK includes temperature monitoring with automatic PWM shutdown at 125°C junction temperature. Ensure adequate airflow in the enclosure; forced air cooling may be required for high-power continuous operation. The MCP9700 sensors provide accurate board temperature monitoring for thermal management algorithms.
faeInsights.highlight: Implement thermal derating at 80°C; ensure adequate heatsinking for continuous high-power operation

faqs.question: What motor types are supported by this solution?
faqs.answer: This solution supports 3-phase brushless DC (BLDC) motors and permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSM). Both sensored (Hall effect) and sensorless configurations are supported. The FOC algorithm works best with PMSM motors having sinusoidal back-EMF, while trapezoidal BLDC motors can use six-step commutation. Supported motor parameters: 4-16 pole pairs, 0.1-10A rated current, 100-15,000 RPM operating range. For motors outside these specifications, contact our FAE team for customization guidance. The software includes motor parameter auto-detection to simplify setup with different motors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Works with BLDC and PMSM motors; FOC recommended for PMSM, six-step for trapezoidal BLDC.

faqs.question: How do I tune the FOC parameters for my motor?
faqs.answer: Use the Motor Control Tuning GUI included with MPLAB X: 1) Connect the board and run the motor parameter measurement tool to automatically identify Rs, Ld, Lq, and Ke; 2) Set current loop PI gains starting with Kp=10, Ki=100 and adjust based on step response; 3) Set speed loop gains with Kp=0.1, Ki=1 and tune for desired transient response; 4) Configure the sensorless observer parameters based on motor inertia and load characteristics; 5) Use the real-time scope to verify current waveforms are sinusoidal. The GUI provides automated tuning wizards for common motor types. For custom motors, our FAE team can provide tuning support and parameter optimization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use automated parameter measurement; start with conservative gains and increase gradually while monitoring stability.

faqs.question: What safety features are included?
faqs.answer: The solution includes comprehensive safety features: 1) Hardware overcurrent protection via analog comparator with <1μs response time; 2) Overvoltage protection with automatic PWM shutdown; 3) Undervoltage lockout prevents operation with insufficient bus voltage; 4) Thermal monitoring with programmable derating and shutdown thresholds; 5) Watchdog timer ensures software integrity; 6) Fault diagnostic output via CAN bus; 7) Safe state configuration (all gates off) on fault detection. For functional safety applications (ISO 13849, IEC 61800), the dsPIC33CK supports dual-core lockstep operation and ECC memory. Contact our functional safety team for safety manual and FMEDA documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Hardware protection <1μs response; contact FAE for functional safety certification support.

faqs.question: Can I use this for high-voltage motor applications?
faqs.answer: The standard reference design supports up to 48V DC bus voltage suitable for low-voltage industrial and EV applications. For high-voltage applications (110V, 220V, or 400V AC), significant modifications are required: 1) Replace gate driver with high-voltage isolated drivers; 2) Use high-voltage MOSFETs or IGBTs rated for the DC bus voltage; 3) Implement isolated current sensing; 4) Add input filtering and PFC front-end for AC supply; 5) Ensure adequate creepage and clearance distances on PCB. Microchip offers reference designs for 400V motor drives using the same dsPIC33CK controller. Contact our FAE team for high-voltage motor control solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard design up to 48V; contact FAE for high-voltage (110V-400V) motor control solutions.

faqs.question: How do I integrate this with my industrial network?
faqs.answer: The solution includes CAN 2.0B interface using the MCP2562 transceiver. For industrial automation, implement CANopen or Modbus protocols on top of the CAN physical layer. The dsPIC33CK includes dedicated CAN controller with 32 message buffers. For Ethernet-based networks, add the LAN8740 PHY and implement EtherCAT or PROFINET using the dsPIC33CK's high-speed PWM and capture modules. The software stack includes example implementations of common industrial protocols. For integration with PLCs, map motor control parameters (speed, torque, status) to standard process data objects. Our FAE team can provide protocol stack integration support.
faqs.decisionGuide: CAN interface included; contact FAE for EtherCAT, PROFINET, or custom protocol implementation.

solutions.name: Automotive Electronics Solution 2

solutions.title: Automotive Gateway and CAN FD Solution
solutions.description: Automotive-grade gateway solution with CAN FD support, Ethernet connectivity, and secure boot for next-generation vehicle networks.
solutions.longDescription:
The Automotive Gateway and CAN FD Solution from Microchip provides a robust platform for next-generation vehicle network architecture. This solution addresses the growing complexity of automotive electronics by enabling seamless communication between multiple vehicle domains including powertrain, chassis, body electronics, and infotainment systems.

At the core is the SAM E70 series ARM Cortex-M7 microcontroller running at 300MHz, providing the processing power needed for protocol translation, data filtering, and secure firmware updates. The solution supports up to 8 CAN FD channels with 5Mbps data rates, enabling high-bandwidth communication for ADAS and autonomous driving applications.

The integrated Ethernet switch with TSN (Time-Sensitive Networking) support enables deterministic communication for critical control systems. Hardware security features including secure boot, cryptographic acceleration, and tamper detection protect against cyber threats. The solution meets ASIL-B functional safety requirements with ECC memory, clock monitoring, and redundant safety mechanisms.

Comprehensive software support includes AUTOSAR MCAL drivers, CAN protocol stacks (CANopen, J1939), and cybersecurity libraries. The reference design includes schematics, PCB layout, and production-tested firmware. BeiLuo's automotive FAE team provides integration support for OEM and Tier 1 suppliers.
features: 8x CAN FD channels supporting 5Mbps data rates
Hardware security module with secure boot
benefits: High-speed CAN FD reduces bus loading and improves response time
Hardware security protects against cyber attacks
Functional safety compliance for critical systems
Scalable design supports gateway, domain controller, or zone controller

faeInsights.title: CAN FD Migration Strategy
faeInsights.content: When migrating from CAN 2.0 to CAN FD, implement a phased approach: 1) Upgrade gateway first to support both protocols; 2) Migrate high-bandwidth nodes (ADAS, powertrain) to CAN FD; 3) Keep body electronics on CAN 2.0 initially; 4) Use the SAM E70's dual-mode capability for seamless bridging. The gateway can filter and translate between CAN 2.0 and CAN FD networks. Plan for increased bus loading during transition - CAN FD's higher data rates actually reduce bus utilization for the same data throughput.
faeInsights.highlight: Implement gateway-first migration strategy; use dual-mode capability for seamless CAN 2.0 to CAN FD transition

faeInsights.title: Cybersecurity Implementation
faeInsights.content: The ATECC608A secure element provides hardware-based security essential for modern automotive gateways. Implementation best practices: 1) Store all cryptographic keys in the secure element, never in Flash; 2) Use secure boot to verify firmware signature before execution; 3) Implement encrypted CAN communication using the secure element's AES engine; 4) Enable tamper detection with secure key destruction; 5) Use unique device certificates for authentication. The SAM E70's TrustZone can isolate safety-critical code from non-secure applications.
faeInsights.highlight: Use ATECC608A for key storage; implement secure boot and encrypted communication for cybersecurity compliance

faqs.question: What is the difference between CAN 2.0 and CAN FD?
faqs.answer: CAN FD (Flexible Data-rate) is the next generation of CAN protocol with two key improvements: 1) Higher data rates - CAN FD supports up to 5 Mbps during the data phase (vs 1 Mbps max for CAN 2.0); 2) Larger payload - CAN FD supports up to 64 bytes per frame (vs 8 bytes for CAN 2.0). The arbitration phase remains at standard CAN speeds for compatibility. This means CAN FD can transfer 8x more data in the same time, or transfer the same data using less bus bandwidth. CAN FD is backward compatible at the physical layer but requires new controllers and transceivers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use CAN FD for new designs requiring high bandwidth; maintain CAN 2.0 compatibility during migration via gateway translation.

faqs.question: How do I implement secure boot on the SAM E70?
faqs.answer: Secure boot implementation on SAM E70 involves: 1) Generate RSA key pair - private key kept secure, public key programmed into device OTP; 2) Sign firmware image with private key using Microchip's signing tools; 3) Configure boot ROM to verify signature before execution; 4) Store signature and public key in dedicated secure memory; 5) Enable tamper detection to prevent physical attacks. The ATECC608A can store keys and perform verification, offloading the main CPU. On boot, the ROM verifies firmware signature - if invalid, device enters safe mode. This prevents execution of unauthorized or corrupted firmware.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Microchip's secure boot tools; store keys in ATECC608A; enable tamper detection for production systems.

faqs.question: What is ASIL-B and why is it important?
faqs.answer: ASIL (Automotive Safety Integrity Level) is a risk classification system defined in ISO 26262 for automotive functional safety. ASIL-B is the second-lowest level (B, A, C, D with D being highest). It applies to systems with moderate safety risk where failure could cause injury but not life-threatening. ASIL-B requires specific hardware and software safety mechanisms: ECC memory for data integrity, clock monitoring for timing integrity, watchdog timers for software monitoring, and safe state management. The SAM E70 includes hardware features that support ASIL-B compliance including dual-core lockstep, ECC, and safety monitor. Certification requires documentation of safety requirements, failure analysis (FMEDA), and testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: ASIL-B suitable for body electronics and non-critical chassis systems; contact FAE for ASIL-C/D solutions.

faqs.question: Can this solution support AUTOSAR?
faqs.answer: Yes, the SAM E70 is fully supported by AUTOSAR Classic Platform. Microchip provides MCAL (Microcontroller Driver Layer) drivers for all peripherals including CAN FD, Ethernet, ADC, and timers. The drivers are certified to AUTOSAR 4.4 standards and include: CAN driver with CAN FD support, Ethernet driver with TSN support, SPI and I2C drivers, ADC driver with DMA support, PWM and ICU drivers, Watchdog driver, and Flash driver for EEPROM emulation. The solution has been integrated with major AUTOSAR stacks including Vector, EB, and ETAS. BeiLuo can provide AUTOSAR integration support and training.
faqs.decisionGuide: MCAL drivers available for AUTOSAR Classic; contact FAE for AUTOSAR Adaptive or specific stack integration.

faqs.question: How do I implement over-the-air firmware updates?
faqs.answer: Over-the-air (OTA) firmware updates require a secure bootloader and update mechanism: 1) Implement dual-bank Flash with active and update partitions; 2) Download encrypted firmware image to update partition; 3) Verify image signature using ATECC608A before activation; 4) Validate image integrity with CRC/SHA; 5) Atomic switch to new firmware with rollback capability; 6) Confirm successful boot before erasing old firmware. The SAM E70's 2MB Flash supports dual-bank operation. Use the secure element for signature verification and encryption. Implement UDS (Unified Diagnostic Services) over CAN or DoIP (Diagnostic over IP) for update communication. The reference design includes a complete OTA implementation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use dual-bank Flash for safe updates; implement signature verification with ATECC608A; use UDS or DoIP for communication.
